summary: Iteration 2 of the Dashboard UI design resolves all five findings from iteration 1, and the design is now aligned with guides/backend-guide.md and guides/ui-style-guide.md. F1: HomeController#safely remains a narrow `rescue StandardError`, but §4 now proposes the accompanying backend-guide amendment (a scoped 'presentation-boundary rescue' sanctioned only for read-only query objects, one call per rescue), satisfying CLAUDE.md's 'propose a guide addition in the same PR' requirement exactly as §6/§8 do for their deviations. F2: the query reads now execute in the controller inside the `safely` boundary (`.new(current_user).call`), materialize into a `Data` struct / Event array / hash, and views read plain attributes only — data access is back in the controller/PORO layer and query failures fall within the rescue. F3: all eight Dashboard::Broadcast call sites are audited for after-commit ordering; the three transaction-wrapped sites (approve, manager_tick#record_decision, rework_to_phase#record_rework_event) place the broadcast after the transaction closes, per each file's own convention and the guide's persist->enqueue->broadcast rule. F4: the modal error region now uses the semantic red soft-badge token (bg-red-50 text-red-700 ring-red-600/20) instead of the reserved amber, with a sound rationale for a form-level banner over per-field captions. F5: off-scale mt-1 corrected to mt-2, with an instruction to keep all spacing on the 2/4/6/8/12 scale. Broader checks also pass: thin controllers (index is delegate-only; answers branches solely on result.success?), services/queries as the primary Minitest unit with thin controller and critical-flow system tests, no business-logic model callbacks, service naming consistent with the existing BroadcastCard/BroadcastColumn convention, the Tailwind type scale and semantic status colors, and dom_id-targeted per-user Turbo streams that render true state on load (streams as enhancement). One non-blocking observation, left for Build: the recent-activity feed uses a whole-panel Turbo replace, a self-declared exception to the UI guide's 'target the smallest DOM unit' rule; its justification ('a synthesized read model with no single record owning the view') maps directly onto the proposed §8 aggregate-view amendment, so it is treated as adequately licensed rather than a defect — the Implementer could optionally extend that amendment's wording to name the panel-replace case explicitly.
